LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF JANICE MENDENHALL'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT WITH ORDER IS DENIED. THE MOTION APPEARS TO BE SEEKING A DEFAULT AND A DEFAULT JUDGMENT. DEPARTMENT 302 DOES NOT ENTER DEFAULTS AND DEFAULT JUDGMENTS. IN ADDITION, THERE IS NO INDICATION THAT THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT WERE PROPERLY SERVED ON DEFENDANT IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP 416.50(A). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 430.10(E). CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-02-2015 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Requesting An Order For Settlement On Deft'S
Off Calendar Feb-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff JANICE MENDENHALL, Tel No. (415) 875-0274 appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Argument presented by Plaintiff. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JANICE MENDENHALL'S Motion Requesting An Order For Settlement On Defendants is denied. It is unclear what relief plaintiff seeks and what is the statutory or legal basis for the motion. If plaintiff is seeking reconsideration of the court's prior rulings, the motion is untimely. The court lacks the power to reconsider those prior rulings because judgments in favor of the defendants have been entered. See Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2014 Continued To Feb-18-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT STEVE ARCELONA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The Court previously sustained Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC"), holding that the SAC "does not plead facts establishing Plaintiff complied with the mandatory claims requirement of the Government Claims Act. (Gov. Code sec. 945.4.)" "[A] cause of action against a public employee'for injury resulting from an act or omission in the scope of his employment as a public employee is barred if an action against the employing public entity for such injury is barred." (Gov. Code sec. 950.2.) The Court signed the proposed order that was submitted by email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF JANICE MENDENHALL's motion For Judgment With Order is denied. The motion appears to be seeking a default and a default judgment. Department 302 does not enter defaults and default judgments. In addition, there is no indication that the summons and complaint were properly served on Defendant in accordance with CCP 416.50(a). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
